key_drivers: Rising real yields (DFII10 at 1.91%, up from ~1.5% in early 2026) are the dominant headwind for gold and tech-heavy indices.
Crude oil remains the standout long: inventory draw, tightening supply, geopolitical risk, and slowing growth all aligned bullish.
BoJ hawkish hold and ECB expected June hike are widening rate differentials vs. the Fed, supporting JPY and EUR on a structural basis.

stale_sources: FRED DFII10 data from 2026-04-20 (1 business day lag - acceptable)
FRED BAMLH0A0HYM2 data from 2026-04-20 (1 business day lag - acceptable)
EIA crude oil inventory data from week ending 2026-04-10 (12 days old - stale, confidence reduced for CL)
Gold ETF flows from week ending 2026-04-17 (5 days old - slightly stale)
fallbacks_used: TradingView TVC:MOVE for MOVE index (primary: Yahoo Finance)
TradingView NASDAQ:SOX for SOX index (primary: Yahoo Finance)
TradingView COMEX:HG1! for copper (primary: Investing.com - blocked)
News search for ECB, RBA, BoJ stances (primary: direct central bank sites - limited access)
News search for gold ETF flows (primary: World Gold Council - login required)
